title: Jasione carionii
intro: Jasione carionii, also known as Carion's knawel or Spanish knawel, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Jasione carionii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Jasione carionii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Jasione carionii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It belongs to the genus Jasione, which is a group of small, annual plants. Jasione carionii is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. The plant produces blue or white flowers and is sometimes referred to as the 'Mediterranean bluebell'. Jasione carionii is considered an endemic species, meaning it is found only in a specific geographic region.
